candidates.name: The Ant Is the Pure Empiricist
candidates.description: The ant collects and stores without transforming. Bacon's archetype for pure empiricism: data accumulation without synthesis. Maps onto data hoarding, measurement without theory, the researcher who gathers endlessly but never publishes. The critique is that collection without transformation is not yet knowledge.

candidates.name: The Spider Is the Pure Rationalist
candidates.description: The spider spins webs from its own substance. Bacon's archetype for pure rationalism: theory-building from first principles without empirical grounding. Maps onto armchair philosophy, unfalsifiable frameworks, the theorist who never tests. Elegant but disconnected from reality.

candidates.name: The Honeybee Is the Ideal Scientist
candidates.description: The bee gathers from the world AND transforms through digestion. Bacon's ideal: empirical collection plus rational synthesis. Maps onto the scientific method itself, design thinking (research + prototyping), curation (selection + arrangement), and ML pipelines (data collection + model training). The most productive of the three archetypes because it names the integration that makes knowledge.

candidates.name: Idols of the Tribe

archive_refs.novum_organum: Book I, Aphorisms XXXIX and XLI

candidates.description: The mind as 'false mirror' -- systematic distortions built into human nature itself. Bacon's term for what we now call cognitive biases. The tribe is the human species; these idols afflict everyone. Anticipates confirmation bias, anchoring, and the availability heuristic by 350 years.

candidates.name: Idols of the Cave

archive_refs.novum_organum: Book I, Aphorisms XXXIX and XLII
archive_refs.plato_allusion: Explicit reference to Plato's cave allegory

candidates.description: Each person's private 'cave or den' that refracts the light of nature. Individual biases from temperament, education, and habit. Bacon deliberately echoes Plato's cave allegory but personalizes it: not humanity trapped in one cave, but each person trapped in their own. Maps onto filter bubbles, specialization blindness, and the curse of expertise.

candidates.name: Idols of the Marketplace

archive_refs.novum_organum: Book I, Aphorisms XXXIX and XLIII

candidates.description: Language distorts thought through 'the ill and unfit choice of words.' The marketplace is where people trade words as commerce; imprecise terms 'force and overrule the understanding.' Anticipates the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis, Wittgenstein's language games, and modern concerns about jargon, buzzwords, and euphemism as cognitive obstacles.

candidates.name: Idols of the Theatre

archive_refs.novum_organum: Book I, Aphorisms XXXIX and XLIV

candidates.description: Received philosophical systems as 'stage plays, representing worlds of their own creation after an unreal and scenic fashion.' Dogma as performance. Maps onto paradigm lock-in, cargo cult science, and the way intellectual fashions ossify into orthodoxy. The theatrical metaphor implies that the audience mistakes the performance for reality.
